system_description.overview: Talentify is an AI-powered recruitment marketing and compliance platform that automates multi-channel job advertising, candidate screening, and scheduling. It integrates with client ATS/CRM systems and features Lila, an AI-powered contingent recruiter for voice, text, and omni-channel candidate engagement.

products.Talentify Recruitment Marketing Platform: End-to-end recruitment advertising automation for high-volume hiring with multi-channel job distribution across programmatic job boards, Indeed, Google Search & Display, and targeted social media. Includes campaign analytics dashboard with budget control and cost-per-application optimization.
products.Lila - AI-Powered Contingent Recruiter: Voice, text, and omni-channel AI recruiter capable of first-level candidate screening, automated scheduling with warm transfer to live recruiters, continuous candidate engagement to reduce no-shows, and AI-powered sourcing campaigns leveraging first-party and programmatic data.
products.Compliance & Security Features: Built-in guardrails for regulation-compliant AI interactions, TCPA-compliant texting infrastructure, secure US-based LLM infrastructure with no model training on client data, and automated bias and fairness monitoring.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out - AWS controls excluded from scope, subservice organization)
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices in scope; fully cloud-based. Headquartered at Orlando, FL.

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway with firewalls configured (referenced in CC6.6.1)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings. Intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN).
network_architecture.tls: TLS encryption for all web-based user requests, using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority.
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server.
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic flows through Cloudflare, then into AWS VPC environment with segmented VPCs and ACLs. Remote admin access via bastion host + VPN. Production servers at AWS maintain failover capabilities across multiple availability zones. Network diagram shows Cloudflare CDN at ingress, multiple EC2 instances, ECS containers, RDS databases, S3 storage, and NLP/ML services within the VPC.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (visible from network diagram: EC2 instances, ECS containers, NLP/ML services)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4 confirms changes developed and tested in separate environment before implementation)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM / Password policy of AWS
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA enforced for all remote access (CC6.1.1 test). MFA required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3). MFA required for email access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS Password Policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied by CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider key management service. Access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only (CC6.1.9).

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (not named; likely GitHub based on branch/merge workflow references)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production environment requires additional approval (CC8.1.2). Approval process followed prior to merging to master branch.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code implied - baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code and configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews by CTO. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance. OS patches managed and applied as they become available.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (not named) - generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met for system capacity and performance
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not explicitly named - logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Incident management process invoked for confirmed events and anomalies.

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)
CDN / content delivery and DDoS protection (visible in network diagram)
Job board distribution for recruitment advertising
Programmatic advertising channel for job distribution
AI/ML capabilities for Lila AI recruiter with no model training on client data

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution with antivirus, anti-malware, and Trojan protection. Anti-virus installed on all desktops, laptops, and production servers. Virus definitions updated daily from vendor. Production server definitions updated weekly. Anti-virus and malware protection also configured for Mac-based workstations and laptops.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications. Frequency adjusted as required. Internal IT Infrastructure team performs scans. Self-assessment scan of vulnerabilities performed prior to every release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner and added to risk register.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities: could not be tested because there were no significant changes made during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 / CC6.5.2 - Cybersecurity insurance: could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination: could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Incident response: could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS responsible for physical security of data centers; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Talentify does not have an office; operates from working space managed by building management. Environmental controls managed by building management / AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually to review key internal control measures, risk assessments, and compliance frameworks
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meets weekly to review security activities and processes; governed by a dedicated charter
Formal vendor management policy with quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports, and annual SOC report reviews for vendors with reports
Board of Directors has independent members and meets regularly to review strategic direction, risk management, and compliance
Configuration management tool used for IaC with rollback capability for any approved configuration change
Bias monitoring and fairness features aligned with NY Local Law 144 and Colorado legislation

infrastructure.rationale: Single-cloud AWS with multi-AZ failover and VPC segmentation is solid. Cloudflare at the edge adds a layer. However, no multi-region capability disclosed, specific AWS regions not named, and no CDN/serverless details beyond the network diagram. IaC is implied but tool not named.

application_architecture.rationale: Hybrid pattern with EC2 and ECS containers visible in diagram, plus NLP/ML services. Branch protection and code review enforced. However, no programming languages, frameworks, or API style disclosed. No mention of testing practices or CI/CD pipeline specifics.

data_layer.rationale: RDS, ElastiCache, and S3 visible in network diagram. Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration. Data classification and retention policies in place. Per-tenant segregation confirmed (CC6.1.3). No data warehouse or advanced data architecture disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong security foundation: MFA on production/remote/source code access, IDS/IPS, firewalls on app gateway, annual pen testing by third party, monthly vuln scans, endpoint protection with AV/anti-malware, server hardening, encryption at rest and in transit, cybersecurity insurance, content filtering via proxy. No WAF explicitly named. SIEM tool not named. Good overall but lacks some specificity on tooling.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with configuration management and rollback capability. Branch protection with merge approval required. Separate dev/test/prod environments. Emergency change process documented. However, no CI/CD tool named, no source control platform named, container orchestration via ECS but no details on pipeline automation.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of DR plans with different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover with ability to spin up servers across all AZs. Daily backups with alerts for failures. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, and DR section in Section 5 is brief.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Primary dependency on AWS as sole cloud provider (critical). Cloudflare for CDN. US-based LLM provider for AI capabilities. Integration with Indeed and Google for ad distribution. Formal vendor management program with quarterly assessments. However, heavy concentration on AWS with no disclosed redundancy, and specific vendors beyond AWS and Cloudflare are not named.

overall.rationale: Talentify demonstrates a solid compliance foundation for a startup-scale recruitment tech company. Unqualified opinion with zero exceptions is strong. Security controls are well-structured with MFA, IDS/IPS, encryption, pen testing, and vuln scanning. The main gaps are in specificity: most tooling is undisclosed (no named CI/CD, monitoring, SIEM, languages, or frameworks), no multi-region capability, and no stated RTO/RPO. The network diagram reveals more technical detail than the text (ECS, RDS, ElastiCache, S3, Cloudflare), which is common for a first SOC 2 with a short 3-month observation period.

red_flags: Very short audit period (3 months: May-July 2025) - suggests this is likely their first SOC 2, limiting the depth of control testing
No RTO/RPO disclosed anywhere in the report despite having a BCDR policy
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded as 'not relevant' despite handling sensitive candidate PII and AI-based screening decisions

Multiple controls could not be operationally tested due to no events occurring during the short engagement period (incident response, cybersecurity insurance, customer data deletion, personnel changes)
yellow_flags: No specific technology vendors/tools named for monitoring, SIEM, CI/CD, source control, or logging - makes independent verification difficult
No multi-region failover capability disclosed; single-region with multi-AZ only
No WAF explicitly mentioned despite having internet-facing applications
LLM provider not named - only described as 'US-based enterprise-grade' with no model training on client data
No SSO mentioned for customer or internal access
Auditor firm (Accorp Partners, PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383) is not a well-known Big 4 or major regional firm
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with zero exceptions across all tested controls
MFA enforced across production, remote access, source code, and email
Comprehensive endpoint protection with AV/anti-malware on all servers, desktops, and Mac devices
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party with remediation tracking
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with frequency adjustment capability
Strong governance: Board of Directors with independent members, RGEC (semiannual), ITLC (weekly)
Formal vendor management program with quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors
IaC with configuration management and rollback capability
Branch protection requiring merge approval for production deployments
Data classification policy and per-tenant segregation confirmed
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and failure alerts
Content filtering via proxy server for all systems
Bias monitoring features aligned with emerging AI hiring regulations (NY Local Law 144, Colorado)
key_observations: The network diagram (page 20) reveals significantly more technical detail than the narrative text - showing Cloudflare CDN, multiple EC2 instances, ECS containers, RDS databases, ElastiCache, S3 buckets, and NLP/ML processing services. This suggests a more sophisticated architecture than the text alone conveys.
Talentify uses US-based LLM infrastructure with explicit no-training-on-client-data policy, which is a differentiator for AI-powered recruitment given EEOC and state-level AI hiring law scrutiny.

The company explicitly excludes Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria despite being in the AI recruitment space handling candidate PII - this is a gap that enterprise buyers will likely question.
The CTO (Lucas Macedo Magroski) is the signing authority, not the CEO - common in tech-heavy startups where the CTO owns the security program.

Despite having a 'DevOps & Cybersecurity Specialist' role defined, the report never names any specific DevOps tooling, suggesting the security program may be more policy-driven than tooling-driven at this stage.
The 3-month observation period (May-July 2025) with an August 2025 report date indicates this is very likely Talentify's first SOC 2, done quickly to satisfy customer or investor requirements.
